title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal risk. However, if improperly configured or used without proper validation, it could lead to false positives/negatives or expose sensitive dependency data to attackers.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment before deploying it in production systems.
Ensure proper access controls and secure configurations are in place to prevent unauthorized access to dependency data.
Cross-verify the tool's findings with other trusted vulnerability databases to ensure accuracy.

title: Nokia SR Linux is vulnerable to a local privilege escalation vulnerability. Successful...
description: Nokia SR Linux is vulnerable to a local privilege escalation vulnerability. Successful exploitation of this vulnerability may allow an authenticated user to execute arbitrary commands with superuser privilege.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Nokia SR Linux allows an authenticated user to escalate privileges and execute arbitrary commands with superuser rights. This could lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, service disruptions, or compromise of critical network infrastructure.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches and updates provided by Nokia for SR Linux as soon as they are available.
Restrict access to the affected systems by implementing strict authentication and role-based access controls.
Monitor system logs and network activity for any signs of unauthorized access or suspicious behavior.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net/sched: fix pedit partial...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net/sched: fix pedit partial COW leading to page cache corruption

tcf_pedit_act() computes the COW range for skb_ensure_writable()
once before the key loop using tcfp_off_max_hint, but the hint does
not account for the runtime header offset added by typed keys. This
can leave part of the write region un-COW'd.

Fix by moving skb_ensure_writable() inside the per-key loop where
the actual write offset is known, and add overflow

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46331.

title: The File Sharing & Download Manager – User Private Files plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to...
description: The File Sharing & Download Manager – User Private Files plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'fldr_ttl' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 2.1.6 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with subscriber-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-10093.

title: The WP Review Slider Pro plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to SQL Injection via the 'curselrevs[...
description: The WP Review Slider Pro plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to SQL Injection via the 'curselrevs[]' parameter of the wpfb_find_reviews AJAX action in versions up to, and including, 12.6.8. This is due to the handler reading $_POST['curselrevs'] raw with no sanitization or type casting, then concatenating each array element directly into a `WHERE id IN ( ... )` clause without quoting and executing via $wpdb->get_results() without $wpdb->prepare(). This makes it possible for authenticated attacker

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands on the database, potentially leading to data exfiltration, data manipulation, privilege escalation, or complete database compromise. This could severely impact website functionality and data integrity.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the WP Review Slider Pro plugin to a patched version (if available) or disable the plugin temporarily to mitigate the risk.
Review and sanitize user input in custom WordPress plugins to ensure proper use of $wpdb->prepare() for database queries.
Conduct a security audit of the WordPress site to identify potential unauthorized changes or data breaches caused by this vulnerability.

title: The Premmerce Dev Tools plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Remote Code Execution via missing...
description: The Premmerce Dev Tools plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Remote Code Execution via missing authorization in versions up to and including 2.0. This is due to the 'generatePluginHandler' function lacking any authorization check before processing user-supplied POST data, combined with the 'createFromStub' function performing unsanitized string substitution of the 'premmerce_plugin_namespace' parameter directly into PHP stub files written to the wp-content/plugins/ directory. An attacker can in

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6933.

title: The Abandoned Contact Form 7 plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized arbitrary post...
description: The Abandoned Contact Form 7 plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized arbitrary post deletion in versions up to, and including, 2.2. This is due to a missing capability check and missing nonce validation in the action__remove_abandoned() function, which is registered to both the wp_ajax_remove_abandoned and wp_ajax_nopriv_remove_abandoned hooks. The handler takes a user-supplied recover_id parameter from $_POST and passes it directly to wp_delete_post() with the force-delete flag set t

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Abandoned Contact Form 7 plugin for WordPress allows unauthorized users to delete arbitrary posts, potentially leading to data loss, defacement, or disruption of website functionality. This could impact website credibility and result in reputational damage.
recommended_actions: Immediately update or replace the Abandoned Contact Form 7 plugin with a secure and actively maintained alternative.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to block unauthorized access to vulnerable endpoints.
Review and restore any deleted posts from backups, and ensure regular backups are in place to mitigate future risks.

title: The Static Block plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference in all...
description: The Static Block plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference in all versions up to, and including, 2.2. This is due to the static_block_content() shortcode handler retrieving a post via get_post() using an attacker-supplied 'id' attribute and outputting its post_content without verifying the post's status (private, draft, pending) or the requesting user's capability to view it. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above,

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-10780.

title: The RTMKit plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization in all versions up to,...
description: The RTMKit plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization in all versions up to, and including, 2.0.7 This is due to the get_submission_content AJAX endpoint lacking a capability check to verify that a user has permission to access the requested form submission data. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to view arbitrary form submissions from other users by iterating the entries_id parameter.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5149.

title: Incorrect default permissions issue exists in Optical Disc Archive Software for Windows 5.5.3 and...
description: Incorrect default permissions issue exists in Optical Disc Archive Software for Windows 5.5.3 and earlier. If this vulnerability is exploited, arbitrary code may be executed with SYSTEM privileges.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-50255.

title: The WP Review Slider Pro plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to SQL Injection via the 'stypes' and...
description: The WP Review Slider Pro plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to SQL Injection via the 'stypes' and 'slocations' parameters of the wppro_get_overall_chart_data AJAX action in versions up to, and including, 12.6.8. This is due to the use of stripslashes() on user-supplied JSON strings prior to json_decode(), which removes the escaping applied by WordPress's wp_magic_quotes; the resulting decoded array values are then concatenated directly into SQL WHERE clauses without parameterization, and the con

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8443.

title: Nokia SR Linux is vulnerable to local privilege escalation vulnerability due to unsanitized...
description: Nokia SR Linux is vulnerable to local privilege escalation vulnerability due to unsanitized format validation. Successful exploitation of this vulnerability may allow an authenticated user to execute arbitrary commands with superuser privileges.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users to escalate privileges and execute arbitrary commands with superuser rights, potentially compromising system integrity and enabling further attacks.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches provided by Nokia for SR Linux systems immediately.
Restrict access to affected systems by limiting user privileges and implementing strict authentication mechanisms.
Monitor system logs for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: The Video Conferencing with Zoom plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in...
description: The Video Conferencing with Zoom plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 4.6.7. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to obtain the site's Zoom SDK API key and a freshly-signed JWT that can be used with the Zoom Web SDK to join any Zoom meeting associated with those credentials without a legitimate invitation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6964.

title: A stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in the CGI program of Zyxel GS1900-48HPv2 firmware...
description: A stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in the CGI program of Zyxel GS1900-48HPv2 firmware versions through 2.90(ABTQ.1)C0 could allow a LAN-based, unauthenticated attacker to exploit the flaw and potentially execute OS commands via a crafted HTTP request.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers within the LAN to execute arbitrary OS commands, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or complete system compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Zyxel GS1900-48HPv2 firmware to the latest patched version, if available, or apply any temporary mitigations provided by the vendor.
Restrict LAN access to trusted devices and implement network segmentation to limit exposure to unauthorized users.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection/prevention systems (IDS/IPS) to detect exploitation attempts.

title: A flaw was found in GNOME localsearch (previously known as tracker-miners) MP3 Extractor,...
description: A flaw was found in GNOME localsearch (previously known as tracker-miners) MP3 Extractor, specifically within the tracker-extract-mp3 component. This heap buffer overflow vulnerability occurs when processing specially crafted MP3 files containing malformed ID3v2.3 COMM (Comment) tags. An attacker could exploit this by providing a malicious MP3 file, leading to a denial of service (DoS), which causes an application crash, and potentially disclosing sensitive information from the heap memory.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in GNOME localsearch's MP3 Extractor could allow attackers to exploit a heap buffer overflow by providing malicious MP3 files, potentially leading to application crashes (DoS) and leakage of sensitive information from memory.
recommended_actions: Update GNOME localsearch (tracker-miners) to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-1766.
Implement strict file validation mechanisms to detect and reject malformed MP3 files before processing.
Monitor systems for unusual crashes or memory leaks that could indicate exploitation attempts.

title: A flaw was found in GNOME localsearch (previously known as tracker-miners) MP3 Extractor. When...
description: A flaw was found in GNOME localsearch (previously known as tracker-miners) MP3 Extractor. When processing specially crafted MP3 files containing ID3v2.4 tags, a missing bounds check in the `extract_performers_tags` function can lead to a heap buffer overflow. This vulnerability allows a remote attacker to cause a Denial of Service (DoS) by triggering a read of unmapped memory. In some cases, it could also lead to information disclosure by reading visible heap data.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in GNOME localsearch's MP3 Extractor can lead to a heap buffer overflow when processing specially crafted MP3 files. This could result in a Denial of Service (DoS) or, in some cases, information disclosure by exposing heap data. While the severity is medium, exploitation could disrupt systems relying on GNOME localsearch for file indexing or metadata extraction.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch or update for GNOME localsearch (tracker-miners) as soon as it becomes available.
Implement file validation and sanitization mechanisms to detect and block malicious MP3 files before processing.
Monitor systems for unusual behavior or crashes related to GNOME localsearch and review logs for signs of exploitation.

title: A flaw was found in the `tracker-extract-mp3` component of GNOME localsearch (previously known as...
description: A flaw was found in the `tracker-extract-mp3` component of GNOME localsearch (previously known as tracker-miners). This vulnerability, a heap buffer overflow, occurs when processing specially crafted MP3 files. A remote attacker could exploit this by providing a malicious MP3 file, leading to a Denial of Service (DoS) where the application crashes. It may also potentially expose sensitive information from the system's memory.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-1765.

title:
Improper host validation in the social login autofill feature in 
Devolutions Remote Desktop...
description:
Improper host validation in the social login autofill feature in 
Devolutions Remote Desktop Manager 2026.2.8 allows an attacker to 
disclose stored social login credentials via a crafted web entry 
pointing to a provider lookalike domain.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12162.

title:
Improper input validation in the SSH Elevate Shell feature in 
Devolutions Remote Desktop Manager...
description:
Improper input validation in the SSH Elevate Shell feature in 
Devolutions Remote Desktop Manager 2026.2.7 allows an authenticated user
 with permission to create or modify a shared SSH entry to execute 
arbitrary commands on a remote SSH host using stored elevation 
credentials via a crafted alternate username and user interaction with 
the Elevate Shell action.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12161.

title: A flaw was found in the GNOME localsearch (previously known as tracker-miners) MP3 Extractor ...
description: A flaw was found in the GNOME localsearch (previously known as tracker-miners) MP3 Extractor `tracker-extract-mp3` component. A remote attacker could exploit this heap buffer overflow vulnerability by providing a specially crafted MP3 file containing malformed ID3 tags. This incorrect length calculation during the parsing of performer tags can lead to a read beyond the allocated buffer, potentially causing a Denial of Service (DoS) due to a crash or enabling information disclosure.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The heap buffer overflow vulnerability in the GNOME localsearch MP3 Extractor could allow remote attackers to cause a Denial of Service (DoS) by crashing the application or potentially leaking sensitive information through malformed MP3 files with crafted ID3 tags.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch or update for GNOME localsearch (tracker-miners) as soon as it becomes available.
Implement robust file validation mechanisms to detect and reject malformed MP3 files before processing.
Monitor systems for unusual crashes or behavior and review logs for potential exploitation attempts.

title: A flaw was found in GnuTLS. The `gnutls_pkcs11_token_set_pin` function, used for changing the...
description: A flaw was found in GnuTLS. The `gnutls_pkcs11_token_set_pin` function, used for changing the Security Officer PIN, can lead to a use-after-free vulnerability. This occurs when an attacker attempts to change the PIN with a NULL old PIN for a token that lacks a protected authentication path.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in GnuTLS could allow attackers to exploit a use-after-free condition when changing the Security Officer PIN, potentially leading to unauthorized access, memory corruption, or application crashes.
recommended_actions: Update GnuTLS to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-42014.
Audit systems using GnuTLS for potential exploitation and monitor logs for unusual activity.
Implement additional authentication mechanisms to mitigate risks associated with PIN changes.
